name: Hospital Operational Readiness Checklist
shortBenefit: Pre-launch readiness for a new or expanded hospital.
intro: Verify that all systems, processes, staff and regulatory requirements are in place before launching or expanding hospital operations.

seo.title: Hospital Operational Readiness Checklist — Pre-Launch Verification | Varada Nexus
seo.description: Free hospital operational readiness checklist. Verify all clinical, infrastructure, regulatory and HR requirements before hospital launch.

items.text: OT, ICU and wards fully fitted and cleaned
Medical gas pipelines tested and certified
Electrical backup (DG set / UPS) tested
Fire suppression and alarm system tested
HVAC / air handling units commissioned and validated
HMS (Hospital Management System) live and tested
All equipment calibrated with operator training done
Pharmacy stocked with essential drug list
Lab set up with QC controls running
Blood bank / cross-matching capability in place (if applicable)
All clinical positions filled or locum arranged
Nursing team inducted and duty roster ready
Support staff (housekeeping, security, F&B) in place
Emergency response team trained (BLS/ACLS certified)
Clinical Establishment licence received
Fire NOC and occupancy certificate obtained
BMW authorisation received
Insurance / TPA empanelment completed for at least 3 payors
Tariff list approved by management
Website, signage and marketing materials ready
Billing and cash collection process tested

assumptions: All critical items must be fully complete before patient admission. Partial items may be acceptable for soft launch if risk-assessed.
disclaimer: This tool provides an indicative estimate for general planning only and is not professional, legal, financial, medical or engineering advice. Reference values are editable defaults that vary by location, vendor, regulation and date. Verify critical figures with a qualified professional. Varada Nexus accepts no liability for decisions made using this tool.

faq.q: What is the most common gap at hospital launch?
faq.a: HMS not fully tested, inadequate nursing staffing and pending BMW authorisation are the three most common gaps that delay launches.

faq.q: Can a hospital do a soft launch before all licences are in place?
faq.a: No — the Clinical Establishment licence and fire NOC are mandatory before any patient can be admitted, with no exceptions.

faq.q: How long before opening should the readiness checklist be started?
faq.a: Start 3–4 months before target opening; critical infrastructure and regulatory items should be tracked 6+ months in advance.
